name: Never defer or skip skill steps without approval
description: When executing any skill, every step must be done in full. Deferring, skipping, or marking items as blocked without explicit user approval is forbidden.
summary: Skills must be followed step-by-step. Any deviation requires user decision. No autonomous deferral, partial completion, or marking blocked. Covers Phase 0 scans, tag steps, retros, routing menus, coverage audits, blocked candidates, partial artifacts.

name: No agent budgets
description: The deep-research allocation formula is a planning floor, not a spending cap. Never treat it as a budget limit.
summary: Allocation formula (D + 3 + floor(D/5)) is a floor for scope estimation, not a spending cap. Remove all budget-used tracking. Report agents completed and running, not remaining vs cap.

name: No artificial caps on synthesis output
description: Don't cap candidate/finding lists in synthesis outputs — show everything, organize for readability, never truncate.
summary: Never truncate candidate or finding lists in synthesis output. Present all candidates organized by tier/type/relevance. User decides what to filter. Complements feedback_no_research_caps and feedback_no_agent_budgets.

name: No blanket count-based labels
description: Avoid using frequency-based terms like singleton as category labels when discussing domain content — describe substance, not count.
summary: Do not use blanket count-based labels (e.g. singleton) as category names. Either describe what makes the item specific, or state the frequency directly. Corrected during T40 vocabulary seeding.

name: No broken widgets or partial tabs
description: Dashboard tabs must ship with full functionality — no Data Unavailable placeholders or minimum-viable stubs.
summary: Dashboard tabs ship complete or not at all. Fix data source gaps before building the tab. Build in order of data readiness. Data Unavailable is acceptable only during development. SoNash-origin, dashboard-specific.

name: Never accept incomplete agent findings
description: Agent results that skip, sample, aggregate, or otherwise leave gaps are unacceptable — dispatch follow-up agents until every item has individual structured verification.
summary: Never silently accept partial agent output. Dispatch fill-in agents for every gap. Always present options to user explicitly. The recommended option when gaps exist is re-dispatch. Exception only if user explicitly chooses skip after seeing the gap stated.

name: feedback_no_preexisting_rejection
description: Pre-existing is not a valid rejection reason in PR reviews — always present fix-or-track options to the user.
summary: Never dismiss PR review items as pre-existing. Regardless of codebase-wide instance count, present user with: (a) fix now + effort estimate, or (b) track as tech-debt. Correction from multiple prior sessions.

name: Never jump to next phase without being asked
description: Don't suggest or start planning/execution/session-end unless the user explicitly asks. Proceed means the current thing, not the next thing.
summary: After completing any phase, present results and ask what next. Do not suggest deep-plan after research, execution after planning, or session-end after execution. Proceed means the approved current thing. When asked anything else, name only true blockers. Never suggest session-end as a deferred-work bucket.

name: No caps on research scope
description: Never cap agent counts or constrain research scope — estimates are fine but no maximums.
summary: Never present max agent counts or scope ceilings in research plans. Estimates for time/effort are fine. If verification or gap-fill needs more agents than allocated, spawn them. Applies to deep-research, deep-plan discovery, and any multi-agent workflow.

name: Never assume session-end
description: Do not reference or assume /session-end as a future commit destination, cleanup step, or fallback unless the user explicitly requests it.
summary: Never propose fold into session-end, defer to session-end, or handle at session-end. Concrete commit options: commit now, leave dirty, discard. If dirty state accumulates, surface and ask. Only mention session-end if user referenced it first in current or very recent turn.

name: No silent skill failures
description: Skills must detect, retry with mitigation, and report failures — never silently skip steps.
summary: After every MUST/SHOULD step, verify expected output exists. If missing: retry with mitigation (re-clone, narrower scope, split agents). If retry fails: report to user with options. Never silently continue. Mirrors deep-research agent overflow pattern.

name: No stale CI assumption
description: Never assume CI failures are stale runs — verify commit SHA in CI logs before dismissing.
summary: Don't dismiss CI failures as stale without verifying the commit SHA in CI logs matches the current HEAD. Only reject as stale if SHA is demonstrably behind the fix commit. Corrected during PR #503 R3.

name: Don't brainstorm when decisions are already made
description: Don't invoke /brainstorm when the user has already decided what to do. Brainstorming is for exploration, not execution planning.
summary: Brainstorm is for creative exploration and discovery. Do not invoke when user says lets do X with clear parameters. Execution tasks with clear instructions have 0% chance of needing brainstorming. Corrected Session #265 2026-04-06.

name: feedback_parallel_agents_for_impl
description: Use parallel agents with convergence loops for implementation — not single-threaded work that leads to implementation theater.
summary: Use parallel agents for implementation, not sequential single-thread. Agent teams for skill changes. Convergence loops to verify implementations actually work. Testing also uses parallel agents. Single-threaded implementation leads to implementation theater.

name: Per-skill self-audit scripted from base
description: Almost every skill should have its own self-audit scripted from a shared base version and adjusted for that skills specifics — distributed quality gate, not a single meta-skill.
summary: Every skill needs its own self-audit script generated from a shared base (skill-audit) then adjusted for skill-specific invariants. Per-skill catches what generic cannot. When porting a skill, include self-audit.md as part of the skill package. When updating skill-audit base, downstream self-audits get re-generated at next audit.

name: feedback_permission_over_aliases
description: Shell aliases have minimal value in director+AI workflow — permissions are the real friction reducer.
summary: Shell aliases save nothing in director+AI workflow because Claude types all commands. Real friction is permission prompts blocking Claude. Fix via granular allow/deny rules in settings.json. When recommending workflow tools, evaluate reduction of permission prompts for autonomous AI work.

name: PR review content comes from user paste, not gh
description: Never fetch PR review content via gh — always wait for the user to paste. gh returns truncated/snapshot data.
summary: PR review source of truth is user-pasted content. gh pr view --json comments returns incomplete snapshots — collapsed sections, truncated bodies, missing inline suggestions. gh pr view --json files and gh pr checks are OK for structural metadata. Never auto-fetch review content. Origin: JASON-OS PR #3 R1 2026-04-17.

name: pr-review must use state files for cross-round data
description: pr-review skill must persist previous round counts in state files so compaction/clear does not lose them.
summary: pr-review warm-up reconstructs from conversation context, which breaks after compaction/clear. State files must persist cumulative per-round data (total/fixed/deferred/rejected). State files must also be git committed with each review round fix commit — cross-locale work otherwise loses context. State file path: .claude/state/task-pr-review-{pr}-r{round}.state.json.

name: PR timing discipline
description: Do not create PRs until ALL planned work is complete — commit and push are fine, but PR creation signals done.
summary: Create PRs only when all planned items are implemented. Commits and pushes along the way are fine. PR creation signals ready. If scope needs splitting, get explicit approval first. Corrected after PR created with 5 of 8 planned items done.

name: Pre-analysis required before every port
description: Every file being ported from any source repo must undergo pre-port analysis first — SoNash refs, upstream callers, downstream dependencies — no exceptions.
summary: Every port requires pre-analysis: SoNash-reference scan, upstream callers, downstream dependencies, port verdict. Output goes to PORT_ANALYSIS.md ledger with one row per file. Prevents research-stage errors becoming porting errors. Applies to JASON-OS and any template-extraction plan.

name: Pre-commit fixer requires report + confirmation
description: After fixing pre-commit failures, present the structured PRE-COMMIT FIX RESULT report and wait for user confirmation before re-committing.
summary: After pre-commit hook fixes, always present structured report (Status, Attempt N/3, Failures fixed, Remaining) before re-committing. Wait for user confirmation. Do not auto-retry the commit. User wants visibility and approval control over commit contents.
